'''Jake Wardle''' (born 18 November 1998) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and [[England national rugby league team|England]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] and has spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League|top flight]]. Earlier in his career he played as a {{rlp|wg}}er.

==Background==
Wardle was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England

He is the younger brother of fellow former [[Huddersfield Giants|Giants]] player [[Joe Wardle]].<ref name="Huddersfield promote England academy international Jacob Wardle to squad.">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/10037549/huddersfield-promote-england-academy-international-jacob-wardle-to-their-full-time-squad|title=Huddersfield promote England academy international Jacob Wardle to squad|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2015-10-21|accessdate=2016-09-12}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Illingworth ARLFC===
Jake began his Rugby League career at amateur club Illingworth ARLFC, before signing professional forms. During this time he played in the England Youth internationals against France.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wardle-joins-his-brother-at-the-giantswardle|title=Wardle joins his brother at the Giants|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
Wardle is a product of Huddersfield's academy system,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/locker-room/profile/819/jake-wardle|title=Huddersfield Giants - Rugby Super League}}</ref> and made his first team début in May 2018, scoring two tries against [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Huddersfield 28-18 Widnes: Chris Thorman ends Giants spell with welcome win |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/huddersfield-vs-widnes/report/48353?keepThis=true&TB_iframe=true&height=650&width=850&caption=SkySports+%7C+News |website=Sky Sports |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=4 May 2018}}</ref>
[[File:Jake Wardle.jpg|thumb|upright|Wardle playing for Huddersfield in 2019]]
In 2020, Wardle signed a new contract to stay at Huddersfield until the end of the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/huddersfield-centre-signs-new-deal-despite-nrl-interest-30060/|title=Huddersfield centre signs new deal despite NRL interest|website=Serious About Rugby League|date=25 May 2020}}</ref>
[[File:Jacob Wardle.jpg|thumb|upright|Wardle warming up for the Wigan Warriors in 2024]]
====Warrington Wolves (loan)====
On 25 June 2022 it was announced that he would join the [[Warrington Wolves]] for the remainder of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toby-king-jake-wardle-warrington-huddersfield-swap/|title=Warrington's Toby King and Huddersfield's Jake Wardle in swap deal|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 June 2022 |accessdate=27 Jun 2022}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
In October 2022 it was announced that Wardle had joined [[Wigan Warriors]] on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2022/10/09/jake-wardle-joins-wigan/ |title=Jake Wardle joins Wigan on 3 year deal|date=9 October 2022|website=Wigan Warriors}}</ref>

In round 25 of the 2023 Super League season, Wardle scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 50-0 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12958070/leeds-rhinos-0-50-wigan-warriors-jake-wardle-propels-wigan-to-win-and-back-top-of-super-league/|title=Leeds Rhinos 0-50 Wigan Warriors: Jake Wardle propels Wigan to win and back top of Super League|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, Wardle played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]]. Wardle was also awarded the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] as man of the match.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

On 24 February 2024, Wardle played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] scoring a controversial try in the second half. The try was sent to the video referee who stated that there was insufficient evidence to show whether the ball had crossed the line however on some replays it showed the ball was short of the line.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 27 March 2024, Wardle signed a five-year contract extension with Wigan to remain at the club until the end of 2029.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68665854|title=Jake Wardle: Wigan Warriors centre signs new five-year deal |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 8 June, Wardle played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup |work=BBC Sport}}</ref> Wardle retained his place in the [[Super League Dream Team]] for the [[2024 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c78de9v0q21o |title=Wigan head up Super League Dream Team selections |work=BBC Sport |date=22 September 2024 |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, he played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=The Guardian}}</ref>
On 30 March 2025, Wardle scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 54-0 win over strugglers Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c17q4e7gpxzo|title=Wigan romp to 10-try win over spirited Salford |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 9 October 2025, Wardle played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On the opening game of the [[2026 Super League season]], Wardle suffered a [[medial collateral ligament]] injury ruling him out for three months<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce3gqnz74zwo |title=Wigan centre Wardle out for up to three months |work=BBC Sport |date=18 February 2026 |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref> in a match where he also scored helping Wigan to an opening round victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c363nd47400o |title=Wigan start with hard-fought win at Castleford |work=BBC Sport |date=15 February 2026 |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, Wardle played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship|title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
